# UC-22.11.67 ¬∑ Comprehensive Audit Trail for Successful CDE Administrator Logons

## Description

Produces a consolidated view of successful interactive and remote logons to CDE admin jump accounts including user ID, time, origin, and object accessed for all individual user access to cardholder systems.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS, PCI-DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes linux, windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security, linux:audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Ensure 4624 includes workstation name and IP fields populated. (2) Mask sensitive columns in shared dashboards. (3) Long retention index or archive for 12+ months. (4) Map to quarterly access review samples. Indexes required: `index=linux`, `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=linux:audit`; Lookups: `pci_cde_servers`, `pci_cde_servers.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` `auditd` type=USER_LOGIN.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Ensure 4624 includes workstation name and IP fields populated. (2) Mask sensitive columns in shared dashboards. (3) Long retention index or archive for 12+ months. (4) Map to quarterly access review samples.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=linux sourcetype="linux:audit" type="USER_LOGIN" earliest=-24h)
| lookup pci_cde_servers.csv coalesce(ComputerName,host) as host OUTPUT in_cde
| where in_cde="true"
| eval user_id=coalesce(TargetUserName,acct)
| eval src=coalesce(IpAddress,addr)
| table _time, host, user_id, LogonType, src, TerminalSessionId
| sort - _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Comprehensive Audit Trail for Successful CDE Administrator Logons (PCI DSS Req 10.2.1, 10.2.2)** ‚Äî Produces a consolidated view of successful interactive and remote logons to CDE admin jump accounts including user ID, time, origin, and object accessed for all individual user access to cardholder systems.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` `auditd` type=USER_LOGIN. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows, linux; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security, linux:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=windows, index=linux, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where in_cde="true"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **user_id** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **src** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Comprehensive Audit Trail for Successful CDE Administrator Logons (PCI DSS Req 10.2.1, 10.2.2)** ‚Äî Produces a consolidated view of successful interactive and remote logons to CDE admin jump accounts including user ID, time, origin, and object accessed for all individual user access to cardholder systems.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` `auditd` type=USER_LOGIN. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, LogonType, TerminalSessionId, host, src, user_id.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sortable audit view), timeline, pivot by user_id.

## SPL

```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=linux sourcetype="linux:audit" type="USER_LOGIN" earliest=-24h)
| lookup pci_cde_servers.csv coalesce(ComputerName,host) as host OUTPUT in_cde
| where in_cde="true"
| eval user_id=coalesce(TargetUserName,acct)
| eval src=coalesce(IpAddress,addr)
| table _time, host, user_id, LogonType, src, TerminalSessionId
| sort - _time
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (sortable audit view), timeline, pivot by user_id.
